back_translation: Paul, prisoner of Jesus Christ, and Timothy our brother, to Philemon, who is beloved and a fellow worker of ours
and Apphia our sister, and Archippus our fellow soldier, and the church that meets at your house
May grace and peace from God our Father and from Jesus Christ the Lord be with you all
I always thank my God while remembering you in my prayers
because I hear of your love and faith which you have toward Jesus the Lord and toward all the saints
I pray that the participation in your faith would bear fruit through full knowledge of every good thing that is in us for Christ
For I have great joy and encouragement in your love, because the hearts of the saints have been refreshed by you, brother
Therefore, although in Christ I have great boldness to command you to do what is proper
I appeal rather on the basis of love — as one who is Paul, an aged man, and now also a prisoner of Jesus Christ
I appeal to you concerning my child, the one whom I begot during my imprisonment — Onesimus
who once was useless to you, but now has become useful both to you and to me
I have sent him back to you — this one is my own heart
I would have liked to keep him with myself, so that he might serve me on your behalf, in my imprisonment for the gospel
But I did not want to do anything without your consent, so that your good deed would not be by compulsion but by free will
For perhaps this was the reason he was separated from you for a short time, so that you might receive him back forever
no longer as a slave, but more than a slave — a beloved brother, especially to me, but how much more to you — both in the flesh and in the Lord
Therefore, if you consider me a partner, welcome him as you would welcome me
But if he has wronged you in any matter, or owes you anything, charge that to my account
I, Paul, have written with my own hand. I will repay — without needing to mention to you that you yourself owe me even more than that
Yes, brother, let me receive benefit from you in the Lord. Refresh my heart in Christ
I write to you with confidence in your obedience, knowing that you will do even more than I ask
At the same time, please prepare a lodging for me, because I hope that through your prayers I will be granted back to you all
Epaphras, my fellow prisoner in Jesus Christ, sends greetings to you
Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke, my fellow workers, also send greetings to you
May the grace of Jesus Christ the Lord be with the spirit of you all
